question_text: A light ray travels through water and hits a diamond surface at an angle of incidence of 25°. The critical angle for the water-diamond boundary is 32°. Explain whether total internal reflection will occur, and describe what will happen to the light ray.
correct_answer: No, total internal reflection will not occur. The angle of incidence (25°) is less than the critical angle (32°), so the light will refract into the diamond and bend away from the normal.
explanation: Total internal reflection only happens when the angle of incidence is greater than or equal to the critical angle. Since 25° is less than 32°, the light has enough energy to pass through the boundary and will refract according to Snell's law.
